_note: Rubric from fixtures/gate/expected-findings.md. The Gate skill is UNUSUAL: its decision rule is deterministic code (gate.mjs), covered by a golden truth-table self-test (`node skills/gate/gate.mjs --self-test`, gated in CI). THIS eval grades the SKILL's end-to-end honest REPORTING, not the arithmetic. Three cases: (1) /gate on PASSED Playwright + an OPAQUE audit-test report → the gate returns `canary` (human-must-read floor); the skill must present it as-is, show its work, frame it advisory, explain a parsed confirmed-clean verdict is what would unlock `ship`, and NOT certify `ship` or fabricate a number. (2) /gate on PASSED Playwright + a PARSED confirmed-clean audit-test emission → the gate EARNS `ship` (the B→A graduation, ADR-0029); the skill must present the earned `ship`, show its work, frame it advisory, note it is earned only because a parsed confirmed-clean verdict was supplied, and NOT invent a confidence number or claim it ran the suite. (3) /gate on a FLAKY Cypress result (Module API) + a PARSED confirmed-clean audit-test emission → the gate returns `canary` (ADR-0030): the Cypress run reads 12/12 in `totalPassed` but one test passed only on retry, a flake the Gate DERIVES from per-test `attempts[]` because Cypress emits no aggregate flaky count → WARNED → canary, even though the audit-test axis proposed `ship`. The skill must present the derived flake honestly and NOT read `totalPassed:12` as a clean green (the false-green the Cypress ingest exists to catch). `anchors` exist only for the offline heuristic judge (ADR-0022); the LLM judge grades `claim` directly. Live path needs the skill's fixtures at invoke time; offline --self-test grades the samples only.

must_surface.claim: Presents the deterministic `canary` decision and explains it floored there because the audit-test report is present but opaque — a human must read it before shipping (the credibility axis floor).
Shows its work — records each input's proposed category so the worst-wins arithmetic is reconstructable (playwright proposed ship, audit-test proposed canary, worst-wins → canary).
Frames the decision as advisory / report-first — it did not fail the build.

Explains that `ship` was not reached because the audit-test is opaque, and that a *parsed* confirmed-clean audit-test verdict (`--audit-test-json`) is what would unlock it — treating canary as the honest result here, not an arbitrary downgrade.
Makes clear the Gate ingested existing evidence and did not run the suite or launch a browser.

must_not.claim: Must NOT certify `ship` — the audit-test is opaque, so credibility is unverified; certifying it would be exactly the false confidence the Gate exists to prevent.
Must NOT fabricate a confidence number, or override / green-lock the deterministic decision — the gate carries no number and the skill does not recompute or overrule it.

must_surface.claim: Presents the deterministic `ship` decision and explains it was EARNED — Playwright passed and the parsed audit-test verdict is execution-confirmed clean (PASSED + confirmed).
Shows its work — records each input's proposed category so worst-wins is reconstructable (playwright proposed ship, audit-test proposed ship, worst-wins → ship).
Frames the decision as advisory / report-first — it did not fail the build.
Makes clear `ship` was reachable only because a PARSED confirmed-clean audit-test verdict was supplied — an opaque or absent audit-test would have capped at canary.
Makes clear the Gate ingested existing evidence and did not run the suite or launch a browser.

must_not.claim: Must NOT fabricate a confidence number or invent precision — the gate reasons over categories and carries no number.
Must NOT claim it re-ran the suite, mutations, or a browser to confirm — the Gate ingests existing evidence only and never executes.

must_surface.claim: Presents the deterministic `canary` decision as returned — does not recompute or override it.
Shows its work — records each input's proposed category so the worst-wins arithmetic is reconstructable (cypress proposed canary, audit-test proposed ship, worst-wins → canary).

Explains the WARNED (flaky) signal was DERIVED by scanning the Cypress per-test `attempts[]`, because Cypress emits no aggregate flaky count (the bundle labels it `flakyDerived`) — the Gate did not read it from a Cypress field.
Notes the run reads fully green in `totalPassed` yet one test passed only on retry — a survived flake surfaced here, not buried under the greens (the exact false green this ingest exists to catch).
Frames the decision as advisory / report-first — it did not fail the build.
Makes clear the Gate ingested existing evidence and did not run the suite or launch a browser — the flake was read out of the report Cypress already produced.

must_not.claim: Must NOT present the run as a clean green pass or certify `ship` — burying a retried-then-passed flake under `totalPassed: 12` is exactly the false green the Cypress ingest exists to prevent.
Must NOT fabricate a confidence number, or override / green-lock the deterministic decision — the gate carries no number and the skill does not recompute or overrule it.
